black.text: My therapist says I have an unhealthy obsession with ___.

A micropenis

Asking a woman if she's pregnant when she isn't

Being marginally less terrible
A passive-aggressive sticky note

An emotional support water bottle

Watching Netflix until 4am and hating yourself
The audacity

A birthday cake with far too many candles

A hot dog with no bun

A motivational poster in Comic Sans
Running away from your problems at 47 mph
Turning down plans and then feeling lonely at home

Rage-donating to a charity
The weird uncle who won't stop talking about cryptocurrency
Simultaneously questioning every life choice
Gasping awake at 3am remembering a mistake from 2009
Performing emotions for social acceptance
The inexplicable confidence of a mediocre man
A Hallmark movie, but with crime
The hidden cost of free things
Loud, public chewing

black.text: My startup is disrupting ___ with blockchain and good vibes.
The production server crashed because someone deployed ___ at 4pm on a Friday.
Our new app monetizes ___ without users knowing.
The AI suggested we fix the memory leak by ___.
The code review was rejected because of ___.
After six sprints, the team delivered ___. Nobody cared.
The intern accidentally deleted ___ from production.
Our entire tech stack is ___, ___, and barely suppressed panic.
Stack Overflow was useless, so we turned to ___.
The CTO blamed ___ for the nine-hour outage.
DevOps merged ___ directly to main.
We're pivoting from ___ to ___ by Q3.
The new engineer can't explain why we still use ___.
According to the ticket, ___ is simultaneously P0 and P5.
We're replacing ___ with AI.
The open source maintainer finally snapped and added ___ to the library.
Ten years from now, developers will struggle to explain why we used ___.
We raised a Series B to build ___. We never shipped it.

A Kubernetes cluster running a static HTML page

git push --force to main at 4:59pm on a Friday
undefined is not a function

'Let's just rewrite the whole thing from scratch'

black.text: My home network went down at midnight because ___.

My new VM is dedicated entirely to ___.
The NAS is full again. This time it's full of ___.
My self-hosted setup now includes ___ and ___ somehow talking to each other.

The server room smells like ___ and poor decisions.
I stayed up all night fixing ___ that didn't need fixing.
My homelab's single point of failure is ___.

white.text: A VM that's been 'temporarily' running for three years
The UPS that beeps every time the power flickers at 3am
Pi-hole blocking my own website
A NAS full of 'Linux ISOs'
Running Minecraft on the same server as Nextcloud

A rack full of blinking orange lights at 2am
Updating firmware at 11pm on a Tuesday for fun
A RAID array that failed during the rebuild
Entering IPs manually for three months because I forgot to set up DNS
VLANs I set up with confidence and never understood again
A Docker volume named after someone I'd rather forget
Self-hosting email and spending 48 hours fighting spam filters
A cron job that runs at 3:14am for no reason anyone can remember
A pfSense config that only I can read, and only barely
A 10-year-old enterprise server in a closet, angry and hot
The dead hard drive that had no backup
A private Gitea instance with one user: me
The network switch that's also load-bearing
Four routers in a trench coat pretending to be one network
A weekend spent automating something that takes 20 seconds manually
Buying SSDs on sale and stockpiling them like a doomsday prepper

black.text: I have logged 3,000 hours into ___ and have absolutely nothing to show for it.
